title: Global liquefied natural gas trade volumes reached record high in 2025
summary: EIA says global LNG trade hit a record in 2025 and U.S. LNG exports rose sharply, supporting LNG infrastructure and gas-exposed assets, while Strait of Hormuz disruptions and sanctions still create supply-chain risk.
claims: Global LNG trade reached a record 56.3 Bcf/d in 2025.
U.S. LNG exports rose 26% to 15.1 Bcf/d and EIA says 2026 flows were slowed by Hormuz-related disruption.

title: U.S. exports of crude oil and petroleum products reached record in April
summary: EIA says U.S. petroleum exports hit a record in April as Hormuz-related disruptions lifted demand for U.S. barrels, but the same geopolitics that support exports can also keep markets volatile.
claims: U.S. petroleum exports reached 13.6 million b/d in April 2026.
Crude, propane, and distillate exports were especially strong.

title: U.S. refining capacity decreased during 2025
summary: EIA says U.S. refinery capacity fell in 2025 after closures in Houston and Los Angeles, which may aid surviving margins but tightens regional fuel availability, especially on the West Coast.
claims: U.S. operable atmospheric distillation capacity fell to 18.2 million b/cd on Jan. 1, 2026.
The report says refinery closures cut West Coast capacity more than Gulf Coast capacity.

title: Metered electricity demand in the New York ISO falls midday because of small-scale solar
summary: EIA says small-scale solar is lowering midday metered demand in New York and steepening the evening ramp, which highlights flexibility value but also complicates balancing for grid operators.
claims: Midday metered demand has fallen as small-scale solar has grown.
The evening ramp has become steeper as solar output fades.

title: U.S. commercial crude oil inventories have decreased in June
summary: EIA says U.S. crude inventories, gasoline, and distillate stocks moved lower or mixed in mid-June, which can support price sensitivity and product spreads but also signals a tighter near-term balance.
claims: Commercial crude inventories fell by 6.1 million barrels in the week ending June 19.
Gasoline and distillate stocks remained below or near five-year averages.

title: UAE's exit from OPEC+ reduced the group's share of crude oil production and capacity
summary: EIA says the UAE's exit from OPEC+ would reduce the group's share of world crude production and capacity, while Hormuz rerouting and pipeline limits keep geopolitics and logistics risk elevated.
claims: Without the UAE, OPEC+'s share of 2025 global crude production would be lower.
The article highlights pipeline rerouting and Hormuz exposure as constraints on exports.

title: Permian natural gas production increased faster than crude oil
summary: EIA says Permian gas output is growing faster than crude oil as the basin matures, supporting takeaway and gas-fired power themes, but rising gas-oil ratios and infrastructure constraints remain a risk.
claims: Permian marketed gas production grew 60% from 2021 to 2025.
EIA expects the gas-oil ratio to keep rising as the basin matures.

title: Data Center Accuracy Report
summary: PJM's data-center load forecast accuracy report says large-load forecasting remains variable and hard to predict, which may improve transparency but also signals planning risk for utilities and developers.
claims: PJM will post an annual accuracy report for the data-center portion of its long-term load forecast.
The presentation says load inputs such as ramp rate and firm versus non-firm status are variable.

title: China's nuclear power capacity nearly doubled since 2016
summary: EIA says China's nuclear fleet kept expanding, with more reactors under construction and a growing domestic supply chain, which supports long-cycle nuclear buildout but still leaves execution and concentration risk.
claims: China's nuclear capacity increased 76% from 2016 to 2024 and rose further in 2025 and through May 2026.
China has 36 reactors under construction and is building its first SMR.

title: Summer Outlook 2026: PJM Prepared To Meet Growing Summer Demand With Adequate Resources
summary: PJM says it expects adequate resources for typical summer peak demand and can lean on demand response, but data-center-driven load growth is tightening reserve margins and raising stress risk during heat or outages.
claims: PJM forecasts sufficient generation for typical peak demand this summer.
It says load growth from data centers is outpacing new generation and tightening reserve margins.

title: Small modular reactors and microreactors under development in the United States
summary: EIA says several U.S. SMR and microreactor designs remain in development for grid, industrial, and data-center use, but high capital costs, licensing timelines, and HALEU fuel availability remain major hurdles.
claims: Several U.S. companies are developing SMR and microreactor designs.
High capital costs, licensing/approval processes, and HALEU fuel needs still constrain deployment.

title: Nuclear reactor restart in Japan will likely displace natural gas electricity generation
summary: EIA says restarting Kashiwazaki-Kariwa Unit 6 should displace some LNG-fired generation in Japan, but the country still relies heavily on LNG imports and restart timing remains sensitive.
claims: The restart should displace natural-gas generation.
Japan still imports large volumes of LNG and TEPCO delayed Unit 7 until 2029-2030.

title: Nuclear plants reported few outages in the first three weeks of January 2026
summary: EIA says U.S. nuclear outages averaged 2.0 GW in the first three weeks of January 2026, supporting baseload reliability, although the Palisades restart still accounts for most current outages and planned maintenance remains seasonal.
claims: Average nuclear outages were 2.0 GW from Jan. 1-21, 2026, below the prior five-year range for most days.
Palisades restart accounted for nearly three-quarters of total U.S. nuclear outages on Jan. 6.

title: PJM Board Outlines Plans To Integrate Large Loads Reliably
summary: PJM says it plans proposals and process changes to integrate new data centers and other large loads while preserving reliability and affordability, but the plan is interim and could still raise consumer-cost and interconnection risk.
claims: PJM outlined actions to integrate new data centers and other large loads.
The plan includes faster interconnection paths, curtailment options, and market reviews.
